data.title: Ukraine Flag NFT Sold For 2258 ETH To Support Humanitarian Efforts
data.description: According to Foresight News, on this day in 2022, an NFT of the Ukrainian flag was sold for 2258 ETH. The proceeds from this sale were donated to Come Back Alive, an organization dedicated to providing supplies to Ukrainian civilians and military personnel.

data.title: A new address withdrew 1,500 BTC from CEX, equivalent to approximately $129 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Lookonchain monitoring, a giant whale created a new wallet address 13 hours ago and withdrew 1,500 BTC (about 129 million US dollars) from Binance.

data.title: CryptoPunk 4464 Sells For Over $2 Million In Ethereum
data.description: According to Foresight News, CryptoPunk 4464 has been sold for 1,011 ETH, which is approximately $2.18 million.

data.title: Planet Morning News
Bybit hackers laundered 62,200 ETH in 24 hours, and the remaining funds may be cleared within three days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Ember Monitoring, since the resumption of money laundering activities at 3 pm yesterday, Bybit hackers have laundered 62,200 ETH (about 138 million US dollars). 
Currently, of the 499,000 ETH stolen from Bybit by hackers, only 156,000 (about 346 million US dollars) remain unlaundered. At the current money laundering speed, it is expected that the stolen funds will be completely cleared in three days.

data.title: Mask Network Founder Issues Ultimatum To Hacker
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on March 2, Mask Network founder Suji Yan issued an ultimatum to a hacker, stating that international law enforcement agencies, including Interpol, the FBI, Hong Kong police, and private security firms, have identified the hacker and are pursuing them. Yan revealed that the hacker's personal information has been fully exposed and that they possess conclusive evidence against the individual.

The hacker has been given 72 hours to return the stolen funds, with the promise of a substantial bounty as an incentive for cooperation. Yan urged the hacker to contact him at suji.yan@sujitech.com and return the funds to the specified address: 0xd96c8525d7199a2aae392adcab5513a09280a03f. Upon the return of the funds, all tracking efforts will cease, collected data will be destroyed, and the case will be closed.

Yan warned that failure to comply would result in escalated enforcement actions, including a global block on the hacker's address and severe legal consequences for the hacker and any accomplices. He emphasized that this is the hacker's only opportunity to resolve the situation amicably.

data.title: Circle Issues 250 Million USDC On Solana Network
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Circle has minted 250 million USDC on the Solana network, as detected by Whale Alert six hours ago.

data.title: Russia Delays Launch Of Digital Ruble CBDC
data.description: According to PANews, the Central Bank of Russia has postponed the launch of its digital ruble central bank digital currency (CBDC), initially scheduled for July 2025. The announcement was made by Central Bank Governor Elvira Nabiullina during a meeting with the Association of Russian Banks (ASROS). Despite the delay, Nabiullina noted that the pilot program is progressing well. She emphasized that the goal is to begin the large-scale implementation of the digital ruble slightly later than planned, after finalizing all pilot details and consulting with banks on the most attractive economic model for their clients, including businesses and individuals. A new timeline has not been established, and the central bank has not mentioned the delay on its website.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 610 USDT with a 3.59% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 02, 2025, 00:36 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 610 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 610.299988 USDT, with a narrowed 3.59% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Microsoft Services Experience Widespread Outages in Major U.S. Cities
data.description: According to PANews, a significant number of users were unable to access various Microsoft programs on Saturday afternoon, U.S. time. Data from Downdetector indicated a surge in reports of outages affecting services such as Microsoft Outlook, Microsoft Exchange, Microsoft Teams, Microsoft 365, and Microsoft Azure after 3:30 PM Eastern Time. Over 37,000 users reported issues with Outlook, around 24,000 experienced problems with Microsoft 365 services, and approximately 150 users faced difficulties accessing their Teams accounts. The outages were most prevalent in New York, Chicago, and Los Angeles, as per Downdetector's findings.
